content.problemStatement:
Chef wants to order food from a food delivery app. He wishes to order once today, and buy three items costing A1,A2A_1, A_2A1‚Äč,A2‚Äč and A3A_3A3‚Äč rupees, respectively. He'll also order once tomorrow, when he'll buy three items costing B1,B2B_1, B_2B1‚Äč,B2‚Äč and B3B_3B3‚Äč rupees, respectively. There is an additional delivery charge of rupees DDD for each order.

He notices that there is a coupon on sale, which costs rupees CCC. If he buys that coupon, the delivery charges on any day, on an order of rupees 150150150 or more shall be waived (that is, the DDD rupees will not be added, if the sum of the costs of the items is ‚Č•150\ge 150‚Č•150).

Note that Chef is ordering the three items together on each day, so the delivery charge is charged only once each day. Also, note that it's only needed to buy the coupon once to avail the delivery fee waiver on both days.

Should Chef buy the coupon? Note that Chef shall buy the coupon only if it costs him **strictly less** than what it costs him without the coupon, in total.

###Input:


The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers DDD and CCC, denoting the delivery charge, and the price of the coupon, respectively.
The second line of each test case contains three space-separated integers A1,A2A_1, A_2A1‚Äč,A2‚Äč and A3A_3A3‚Äč, denoting the prices of the food items that Chef plans to order on Day 111, respectively.
The third line of each test case contains three space-separated integers B1B_1B1‚Äč, B2B_2B2‚Äč and B3B_3B3‚Äč, denoting the prices of the food items that Chef plans to order on Day 222, respectively.
###Output:
For each test case, output **YES** if Chef should buy the coupon, and **NO** otherwise, in a separate line.

###Constraints


1‚Č§T‚Č§1041 \le T \le 10^41‚Č§T‚Č§104
1‚Č§D,C‚Č§1001 \le D,C \le 1001‚Č§D,C‚Č§100
1‚Č§A1,A2,A3‚Č§1001 \le  A_1, A_2, A_3  \le 1001‚Č§A1‚Äč,A2‚Äč,A3‚Äč‚Č§100
1‚Č§B1,B2,B3‚Č§1001 \le B_1, B_2, B_3 \le 1001‚Č§B1‚Äč,B2‚Äč,B3‚Äč‚Č§100

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Testcase 1: On the first day, Chef plans on ordering three food items, costing a total of rupees (100+50+10)=160(100+50+10)=160(100+50+10)=160.   On the second day, Chef plans on ordering three food items, costing a total of rupees (80+80+80)=240(80+80+80)=240(80+80+80)=240.
If Chef buys the coupon he shall save the delivery charge on both days, but the coupon shall cost him rupees 100100100 extra. In total, it shall cost him (160+240+100)=500(160+240+100)=500(160+240+100)=500.
Without the coupon, Chef will have to pay the delivery charges for both days, which shall cost him a total of rupees (160+240+90+90)=580(160+240+90+90)=580(160+240+90+90)=580.
Thus, it's better for Chef to buy the coupon.
Testcase 2: On the first day, Chef plans on ordering three food items, costing a total of rupees (100+100+100)=300(100+100+100)=300(100+100+100)=300.   On the second day, Chef plans on ordering three food items, costing a total of rupees (10+20+30)=60(10+20+30)=60(10+20+30)=60.
If Chef buys the coupon he shall save the delivery charge on only the first day, but the coupon shall cost him rupees 303030 extra. In total, it shall cost him (300+60+30(300+60+30(300+60+30 (coupon cost) +30+30+30 (delivery cost for second day))=420)=420)=420.
Without the coupon, Chef will have to pay the delivery charges for both days, which shall cost him a total of rupees (300+60+30(300+60+30(300+60+30 (delivery cost for first day)+30+30+30 (delivery cost for second day))=420)=420)=420.
Since it shall cost him the same amount in both the cases, Chef shall not buy the coupon.

Explanation:
Testcase 1: On the first day, Chef plans on ordering three food items, costing a total of rupees (100+100+100)=300(100+100+100)=300(100+100+100)=300.   On the second day, Chef plans on ordering three food items, costing a total of rupees (10+20+30)=60(10+20+30)=60(10+20+30)=60.
If Chef buys the coupon he shall save the delivery charge on only the first day, but the coupon shall cost him rupees 454545 extra. In total, it shall cost him (300+60+45(300+60+45(300+60+45 (coupon cost) +30+30+30 (delivery cost for second day))=435)=435)=435.
Without the coupon, Chef will have to pay the delivery charges for both days, which shall cost him a total of rupees (300+60+30(300+60+30(300+60+30 (delivery cost for first day)+30+30+30 (delivery cost for second day))=420)=420)=420.
Since it shall cost him strictly more amount when he buys the coupon, Chef shall not buy the coupon.
